{"id":"bugster-41g.1","title":"Custom @dlt.destination for TaskNotes markdown","description":"Implement the custom dlt destination that writes records to Obsidian TaskNote markdown files.

  @dlt.destination(batch_size=100, max_table_nesting=0)
  def tasknotes(items, table, vault_path=dlt.config.value, tasks_dir='00_Inbox/Tasks'):
      engine = SyncEngine(vault_path, tasks_dir)
      external_items = [_to_external_item(item, table['name']) for item in items]
      engine.sync(external_items)

Key work:
- _to_external_item() mapping per table name (github_issues, jira_issues, linear_issues)
- source_system, external_id, title, status, priority, upstream_modified from dlt record
- Wire into Dagster via dagster-dlt DltSource asset
- Remove IssueService abstraction layer (no longer needed)
- Tests: mock dlt pipeline run, assert TaskNotes created correctly

Reference: https://dlthub.com/docs/dlt-ecosystem/destinations/destination","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"git@edmundmiller.dev","created_at":"2026-02-21T12:11:15Z","created_by":"Edmund Miller","updated_at":"2026-02-21T12:11:15Z"}

new_value: Implemented @dlt.destination in bugster/destinations/tasknotes.py. _to_external_item() dispatches by table name (github_issues/jira_issues/linear_issues) with status+priority mapping per source. SyncEngine wiring unchanged. 11 new tests, 25 total passing.
Closed

{"id":"bugster-4jc","title":"Push path: sync TaskNote changes back upstream","description":"The core differentiator from tnote: detect when a TaskNote's status changes locally and push that change back to the source system (e.g. close a GitHub issue when status → done/cancelled).

IssueService.push() and GitHubService.push() already exist. Need:
- Dagster asset or sensor that scans vault for changed TaskNotes
- Change detection: compare current status to upstream_modified timestamp or a state file
- Route each changed note to the correct service via source_system frontmatter field
- Handle: done/cancelled → close upstream, todo → reopen upstream
- Dry-run flag

Likely approach: git diff on vault (or mtime-based scan) to find recently modified .md files, then check if status frontmatter changed.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"git@edmundmiller.dev","created_at":"2026-02-21T07:40:16Z","created_by":"Edmund Miller","updated_at":"2026-02-21T07:40:16Z"}

{"id":"bugster-41g.3","title":"dlt Jira + Linear sources (supersedes bugster-zxy)","description":"Use dlt-hub verified sources for Jira and Linear instead of hand-rolled adapters.

dlt-hub sources:
  pip install 'dlt[jira]'    # dlt.sources.jira
  pip install 'dlt[linear]'  # or dlt-hub/verified-sources linear

Jira:
  from dlt.sources.jira import jira_issues
  source = jira_issues(subdomain=..., email=..., api_token=...)
  # yields table: jira_issues with fields: id, key, summary, status, priority, updated

Linear:
  from dlt_hub.linear import linear_source  # verify exact import
  source = linear_source(api_key=...)
  # yields table: linear_issues with fields: id, title, state, priority, updatedAt

Work:
  - _to_external_item() mapper for jira_issues and linear_issues table schemas
  - JiraSourceConfig + LinearSourceConfig in config.py: just connection params, no service layer
  - Dagster assets via dagster-dlt
  - Tests: mock dlt pipeline run
  - Close bugster-zxy as superseded

new_value: Replaced GitHubService with @dlt.source in bugster/sources/github.py. Deleted services/github.py and resources/github.py. Factory uses dlt pipeline. 5 new github source tests.

Added bugster/sources/jira.py and bugster/sources/linear.py as @dlt.source wrappers. Pagination, contexts, field flattening. 6 new source tests. Jira+Linear in factory.py. Close bugster-zxy as superseded.
Superseded by bugster-41g.3 — Jira and Linear now implemented as dlt sources.
All three subtasks done: @dlt.destination (41g.1, prev), GitHub source (41g.2), Jira+Linear sources (41g.3). IssueService layer removed. 36 tests passing.
Closed

Fixed: DAGSTER_HOME was unset, causing dagster to hang looking for an instance. Set export DAGSTER_HOME=.dagster/ in Justfile. just sync now completes in ~7s with both assets running in parallel. Also added dagster-dg + dagster-components deps for dg CLI support.

{"notes":"Tool candidates:

macOS: remindctl (https://github.com/steipete/remindctl)
  - Swift CLI, macOS 14+ (Sonoma+), MIT
  - remindctl all --json → JSON array, perfect for dlt
  - install: brew install steipete/tap/remindctl

Linux: remindctl won't work (Swift + macOS Reminders framework)
  - Reminders syncs via iCloud CalDAV (VTODO items)
  - Endpoint: https://caldav.icloud.com
  - Options: vdirsyncer (sync CalDAV → local .ics), then parse VTODO
  - rclone iCloud = iCloud Drive only, NOT Reminders — dead end
  - Cleanest cross-platform path: CalDAV directly via requests/httpx
    (same approach works on both macOS and Linux, avoids remindctl dep)

Recommendation: implement as CalDAV VTODO source (platform-agnostic),
document remindctl as optional fast path on macOS if CalDAV auth is painful."}

{"id":"bugster-9hm","title":"Apple Reminders source","description":"Add Apple Reminders as a dlt source in Bugster, replacing tnote's `[integrations.reminders]` pull.

tnote config (gutted):
  lists = ["Reminders"]
  include_completed = false
  contexts = ["personal"]

Implementation notes:
- Use dlt @dlt.source pattern (see bugster/sources/github.py for reference)
- Reminders accessible via AppleScript or reminders-cli (check what tnote uses)
- Map to ExternalItem with source_system = "reminders"
- Add [[reminders]] source type to BugsterConfig / config.py
- Wire into make_*_asset() factory pattern in assets/factory.py","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"git@edmundmiller.dev","created_at":"2026-02-22T04:32:23Z","created_by":"Edmund Miller","updated_at":"2026-02-22T04:32:23Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"Add Apple Reminders as a dlt source in Bugster, replacing tnote's `[integrations.reminders]` pull.

tnote config (gutted):
  lists = ["Reminders"]
  include_completed = false
  contexts = ["personal"]

Implementation approach: CalDAV VTODO
  Apple Reminders has synced via CalDAV (RFC 5545 VTODO) since forever —
  it's how third-party calendar apps access Reminders on non-Apple platforms.
  This is Apple's own standard iCloud sync protocol, not a hack.

  Endpoint: https://caldav.icloud.com
  Auth: app-specific password (iCloud account settings)
  Items: VTODO components inside VCALENDAR
  Libraries: caldav (PyPI), or raw requests/httpx

  Works identically on macOS and Linux — no platform-specific tooling needed.

  macOS-only alternative: remindctl (https://github.com/steipete/remindctl)
    brew install steipete/tap/remindctl && remindctl all --json
    macOS 14+ only — skip unless CalDAV auth becomes painful.

Implementation tasks:
- Use dlt @dlt.source pattern (see bugster/sources/github.py for reference)
- Map VTODO → ExternalItem with source_system = "reminders"
- Filter by list name (tnote used lists = ["Reminders"])
- Add [[reminders]] source type to BugsterConfig / config.py
- Wire into make_*_asset() factory pattern in assets/factory.py"}
old_value:

    (same approach works on both macOS and Linux, avoids remindctl dep)

Recommendation: implement as CalDAV VTODO source (platform-agnostic),
document remindctl as optional fast path on macOS if CalDAV auth is painful.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"git@edmundmiller.dev","created_at":"2026-02-22T04:32:23Z","created_by":"Edmund Miller","updated_at":"2026-02-22T05:05:21Z"}

{"id":"bugster-z3z","title":"Test Reminders CalDAV sync with real iCloud creds","description":"Add to ~/.config/bugster/config.toml:

```toml
[[sources]]
type = "reminders"
name = "personal"
username = "you@icloud.com"
password = "op://Personal/iCloud App Password/password"
lists = ["Reminders"]
contexts = ["personal"]
include_completed = false
```

1. Generate app-specific password at appleid.apple.com → Sign-In and Security → App-Specific Passwords
2. Add to prod config
3. Run `just sync-prod` and verify TaskNotes appear in vault
4. Close bugster-9hm when confirmed working","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"git@edmundmiller.dev","created_at":"2026-02-22T05:35:20Z","created_by":"Edmund Miller","updated_at":"2026-02-22T05:35:20Z"}

old_value: {"id":"bugster-pmp","title":"bugster writes TaskNotes with invalid frontmatter enums (open/normal)","description":"Bugster-generated TaskNotes use status=\"open\" and priority=\"normal\" but vault lint requires status in {backlog,todo,in-progress,in-review,waiting,done,cancelled} and priority in {none,low,medium,high,urgent}. This blocks pre-commit (tnote task lint) on batch sync output.\\n\\nScope: fix source-\u003eTaskNote mapping so new sync output is valid for vault schema; avoid manual mass-edits.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Repro test fails before fix for open/normal output. 2) Mapping emits valid enums (open-\u003ebacklog, normal-\u003emedium or agreed canonical). 3) Existing tests updated to new canonical values. 4) pre-commit validate-frontmatter passes on generated TaskNotes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"git@edmundmiller.dev","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:02:35Z","created_by":"Edmund Miller","updated_at":"2026-03-09T13:02:35Z","external_ref":"BOT-42"}
